title: cerasus × tamaclivorum
intro: Cerasus × tamaclivorum, also known as the Tama cherry, is a hybrid deciduous shrub derived from the cross between Prunus cerasus (sour cherry) and Prunus fruticosa (bitter cherry). The medicinal properties of the Tama cherry include its high content of anthocyanins, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, and its ability to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and fungi. The health benefits of consuming Tama cherry include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Tama cherry is used in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and overall well-being.
